enonce.texte: Match each relative pronoun with its correct function.

questions.texte: Identify the relative pronoun used for people.
questions.reponse: who

questions.texte: Identify the relative pronoun used for things.
questions.reponse: which

questions.texte: Identify the relative pronoun used for both people and things.
questions.reponse: that

questions.texte: Identify the relative pronoun used to express possession.
questions.reponse: whose

enonce.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct relative pronoun: who, which, that, or whose.

questions.texte: The man ________ lives next door is a doctor.
questions.reponse: who

questions.texte: This is the book ________ I bought yesterday.
questions.reponse: that / which

questions.texte: The girl ________ mother is a teacher is my friend.
questions.reponse: whose

questions.texte: I have a friend ________ can speak four languages.
questions.reponse: who

questions.texte: The car ________ is parked outside is very expensive.
questions.reponse: that / which

enonce.texte: Join the two sentences using a relative pronoun. Pay attention to the correct pronoun choice.

questions.texte: The woman is my aunt. She lives in Douala.
questions.reponse: The woman who lives in Douala is my aunt.

questions.texte: The book is on the table. I borrowed it from the library.
questions.reponse: The book that/which I borrowed from the library is on the table.

questions.texte: The student is very intelligent. Her father is a professor.
questions.reponse: The student whose father is a professor is very intelligent.

questions.texte: I met a man. He works in a hospital.
questions.reponse: I met a man who works in a hospital.

questions.texte: The phone is on the desk. It belongs to my sister.
questions.reponse: The phone that/which belongs to my sister is on the desk.

enonce.texte: Decide if the relative pronoun can be omitted. If so, rewrite the sentence without it.

questions.texte: The book that I read was very interesting.
questions.reponse: The book I read was very interesting. (Pronoun can be omitted because it is the object of the relative clause)

questions.texte: The man who called you is my uncle.
questions.reponse: The man who called you is my uncle. (Pronoun cannot be omitted because it is the subject of the relative clause)

questions.texte: The film which we watched was amazing.
questions.reponse: The film we watched was amazing. (Pronoun can be omitted because it is the object)

questions.texte: The teacher who teaches us is strict.
questions.reponse: The teacher who teaches us is strict. (Pronoun cannot be omitted because it is the subject)

enonce.texte: Find and correct the errors in the following sentences.

questions.texte: The woman which lives next door is friendly.
questions.reponse: The woman who lives next door is friendly.

questions.texte: The book who I bought is very interesting.
questions.reponse: The book that/which I bought is very interesting.

questions.texte: The girl whose she is my sister is here.
questions.reponse: The girl who is my sister is here. OR The girl whose sister I know is here.

questions.texte: The car which it is parked outside is mine.
questions.reponse: The car which is parked outside is mine.

questions.texte: The teacher which teaches English is kind.
questions.reponse: The teacher who teaches English is kind.

intitule: Situation Practice: Describing People and Things

enonce.texte: Describe the following people and things using relative clauses.

questions.texte: Describe your best friend using at least two relative clauses.
questions.reponse: Exemple: My best friend, who is very kind, lives in Yaoundé. She is a person whom I can trust.

questions.texte: Describe your favourite subject at school using a relative clause.
questions.reponse: Exemple: English is the subject that I enjoy the most.

questions.texte: Describe a place you visited using a relative clause.
questions.reponse: Exemple: I visited a city which has many historical buildings.

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (about 80 words) describing your family. Use at least who, which, that, and whose correctly.

questions.texte: Create a text about your family using relative pronouns.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse:

I come from a family who is very close-knit. My mother, who is a nurse, works very hard. My father, whose job is teaching, is very patient. I have a sister that is studying medicine. The house which we live in is located in a quiet neighborhood. I am grateful for the support which my family gives me.

enonce.texte: Imagine you are describing your qualifications for a job interview. Write sentences using relative pronouns to describe your skills, education, and experience.

questions.texte: Describe your qualifications for a job using relative clauses.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse:

I am a student who is passionate about technology. I have skills that are useful in the digital world. My education, which includes computer science, has prepared me well. I have a teacher whose guidance was very valuable. I am looking for a job that will challenge me.

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions.

My Community

I live in a community which is very friendly. The people who live there help each other. My neighbour, whose name is Mr. Smith, is a doctor. He has a clinic which is very well-equipped. There is a school that teaches children from all backgrounds. The teachers who work there are very dedicated. I have many friends whom I play with. The park which is in the centre of the community is a popular place.

questions.texte: Analyze: What relative pronoun is used to describe the community?
questions.reponse: Which (community which is very friendly)

questions.texte: Analyze: What relative pronoun is used to describe the people?
questions.reponse: Who (people who live there)

questions.texte: Analyze: What relative pronoun is used to show possession?
questions.reponse: Whose (neighbour whose name is Mr. Smith)

questions.texte: Create: Write a new sentence describing Mr. Smith's clinic using a relative pronoun.
questions.reponse: Mr. Smith has a clinic which is very well-equipped. OR Mr. Smith has a clinic that is very well-equipped.

questions.texte: Compare: What is the difference between using 'who' and 'that' in the text?
questions.reponse: 'Who' is used for people (teachers who work there), while 'that' is used for things or people in a defining clause (school that teaches children). 'That' can be used for both people and things, while 'who' is specifically for people.
